questions.question: Find the class mark of the class interval 15-25.
The cumulative frequency up to class 20-30 is 15, and up to class 30-40 is 28. What is the frequency of class 30-40?
Calculate the mean using the direct method for the distribution: Classes 10-20 (frequency 4), 20-30 (frequency 6), 30-40 (frequency 5).
Identify the modal class in the distribution: Class 10-20 (frequency 5), 20-30 (frequency 12), 30-40 (frequency 8).
questions.solution: The modal class is the class with the highest frequency. Here, class 20-30 has frequency 12, which is the maximum. Therefore, the modal class is 20-30.

questions.question: Find the median class given total frequency n = 50 and you need to locate where cumulative frequency first exceeds 25.
questions.answer: The median class is the one where CF ≥ 25 for the first time.
questions.solution: The median class is found where cumulative frequency first exceeds n/2 = 50/2 = 25. You would check the cumulative frequencies and identify the class where CF ≥ 25 for the first time.

questions.question: Calculate the mean using the assumed mean method for: Class 0-10 (frequency 5, mark 5), Class 10-20 (frequency 8, mark 15), Class 20-30 (frequency 6, mark 25), with assumed mean a = 15.
Use the step deviation method to find the mean for: Class 100-110 (frequency 3), 110-120 (frequency 7), 120-130 (frequency 5), 130-140 (frequency 2), with a = 115 and h = 10.
Find the mode of the distribution where the modal class is 25-35 with frequency 20, the preceding class has frequency 12, and the succeeding class has frequency 15. Class width h = 10.

Find the median of the distribution with total frequency 40 where the median class is 30-40, the cumulative frequency before the median class is 14, and the frequency of the median class is 8. Class width h = 10.
Create a cumulative frequency distribution for the data: Classes 0-10, 10-20, 20-30, 30-40 with frequencies 5, 8, 12, 5 respectively.
In a distribution, the mean is 35, the median is 36, and the mode is unknown. Use the empirical relationship to find the mode.
questions.solution: Using the empirical relationship: Mode = 3 × Median - 2 × Mean = 3(36) - 2(35) = 108 - 70 = 38.

questions.question: Verify that the empirical relationship Mode = 3 × Median - 2 × Mean holds for: Mean = 30, Median = 32, Mode = 36.
questions.solution: Substituting into the formula: 3 × 32 - 2 × 30 = 96 - 60 = 36, which equals the mode. The relationship is verified.

questions.question: Calculate mean, median, and mode for the distribution: 5-15 (freq 8), 15-25 (freq 12), 25-35 (freq 20), 35-45 (freq 10), 45-55 (freq 5). Compare the three values.

A frequency distribution has the following cumulative frequencies: 10, 25, 45, 60, 72, 80. If the total frequency is 80 and class width is 5, reconstruct the frequency distribution and find the mean using the step deviation method with a = class mark of 3rd class.

For a distribution with 100 observations, identify the median class and calculate the median if CF up to class 20-30 is 35 and CF up to class 30-40 is 62. The frequency of class 30-40 is 27. Class width h = 10.
Construct an ogive (less than type) and use it to find the median graphically for the data: 10-20 (freq 5), 20-30 (freq 8), 30-40 (freq 12), 40-50 (freq 10), 50-60 (freq 5).
questions.answer: The median is approximately 32 (from the ogive at n/2 = 20).
questions.solution: Cumulative frequencies: 5, 13, 25, 35, 40. Plot points: (20, 5), (30, 13), (40, 25), (50, 35), (60, 40). For n/2 = 20, locate on CF axis, draw horizontal to curve, then drop perpendicular to x-axis. The intersection point on x-axis gives the median ≈ 32.

questions.question: In a grouped frequency distribution, the sum of frequencies is 60, and Σ(fi × xi) = 2400. Find the mean. Also, if the assumed mean method gives the same mean with a = 30, find Σ(fi × di).
questions.solution: Mean = Σ(fi × xi) / Σfi = 2400 / 60 = 40. Using assumed mean method: 40 = 30 + Σ(fi × di) / 60, so Σ(fi × di) / 60 = 10, thus Σ(fi × di) = 600.

questions.question: For a grouped distribution, if the modal class is 40-50 with frequency 25, preceding class frequency 15, succeeding class frequency 18, and class width 10, find the mode and identify which measure it represents most accurately.
questions.answer: Mode ≈ 43.48. It most accurately represents the concentration point of the distribution.

questions.question: A frequency distribution has mean 25 using the step deviation method with a = 20, h = 5, and Σ(fi × ui) = 30. Find the total frequency Σfi.
questions.solution: Using step deviation method: 25 = 20 + (30 / Σfi) × 5. So 5 = (30 / Σfi) × 5 ⟹ 1 = 30 / Σfi ⟹ Σfi = 30. Wait, let me recalculate: 25 = 20 + (30 / Σfi) × 5 ⟹ 5 = (150 / Σfi) ⟹ Σfi = 150 / 5 = 30.

questions.question: In a distribution, 30% of observations fall below 25, 70% fall below 40, and 90% fall below 60. Determine whether the distribution is skewed and in which direction by analyzing these percentiles.
questions.solution: The 30th percentile is 25, the 70th percentile is 40, and the 90th percentile is 60. The distances: from 30th to 70th percentile = 40 - 25 = 15, from 70th to 90th percentile = 60 - 40 = 20. Since the upper tail (20) is longer than the lower tail (15), the distribution is positively skewed (has a longer right tail).
